commentary_text: The model gives Mexico a 71% win probability while the sportsbook consensus stands at 49%—a 22-percentage-point gap favoring the model's confidence in a Mexico victory. The market also assigns Czech Republic a substantially higher win probability (26%) than the model does (7%), suggesting real-money flows are pricing in scenarios of either recent form shifts, injury concerns, or lineup changes that the model's last training update may not capture. Both sources agree Mexico is the favorite, but the market's wider distribution across all three outcomes signals material uncertainty about team readiness or personnel that the dashboard flags as worth monitoring before kickoff.
